reasoning: Each block requests 48 KB of dynamic shared memory (`12288 * 4`), and on an A10G/GA102 SM this typically allows only 2 resident blocks/SM; with 256 threads/block that is about 16 warps/SM, i.e. ~25% achieved occupancy, below the 35% threshold. The kernel has many repeated shared-memory and indexed global-memory accesses inside a 256-iteration loop, so it needs warp-level concurrency to hide latency; arithmetic intensity is only on the order of ~1.5K FLOPs versus ~2 KB global traffic per thread (< 1 FLOP/byte), far below the 62 FLOP/byte ridge point, but the likely limiting factor is the low active-warp count imposed directly by the 48 KB shared-memory footprint.
